Pocketing - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

POE - Process of Elimination - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

PR - Power Role - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.

Q
N/A

R
Roleclaim - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

RVS - Random Voting Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

RQS - Random Questioning Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

RWSO - Rand Wolf, Sub Out - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.

S
Scum - Another word for mafia-aligned players

Scumbaiting - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

Scumhunting - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

SOD - Start of Day

Soft Claim - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

Spewing - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).

T
Thunderdome - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

Tinfoil - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

TMI - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

Tunnel - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

TWTBAW - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.

U
N/A

V
VCA - Vote Count Analysis - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

VT - Vanilla Townie - A town role with no special abilities.

Vanilla - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

Villager - Another word for town-aligned player.

W
Wagon - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

WIFOM - Wine In Front Of Me - Term originating from this scene in The Princess Bride movie. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

WIL - Wants It Less - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

WIM - Wants It More - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

WOAT - Worst of All Time

Wolf - Another word for mafia-aligned players.

X
N/A

Y
N/A

Z
N/A


As for how the game gets started:
Somebody should make RQS, which is a series of around five questions designed to get information from the players, mostly not related to the game (i.e. how to refer to a person), but they also can be game-related. Any of you can do it!

Since the main gameplay loop of mafia is entirely dependent on previous posts, to start somewhere it is common to start RVS, wherein you vote people for random reasons until you eventually get enough things that are actually AI (alignment indicative/readable i.e. something that you can say is towny or wolfy)

That was a bunch of theory all at once but just have fun they don't call it a social deduction game for nothing or maybe they do?

tl;dr please post & make reads
post_canonical:
[hide=2/7 (char limit)][b]please read this before you post (if you see it)[/b]
Hi, welcome to the Mafia! forum, my marketing brain is broken so this is all you'll get besides from me today in terms of enthiusasm besides a vague 'mafia is fun' which it is.

You should make sure to read all the rules, and follow them, especially making sure to not edit or delete your posts, or post when it's either night your you are dead.

As for how to play the game:
Mafia is about finding out which players are town, and which ones are mafia. How does this work? Well you look at their posts and you try to see if they're more likely to be a post that town or mafia made. Especially as a newbie it doesn't really matter if you're right so much as that you are making reads. It's really helpful for you to post game-related things, both because it provides interesting ideas into the thread, and it makes yourself more readable, which is something you should want as town. You also want to read all of the posts, which is probably only going to be a few hundred over the course of the game, so that you'll have better reads on each player, and generally understand what is going on.

As for how you tell whether a post is towny/town-indicative or wolfy/scummy/maf-indicative? It's not an exact science, but here are some common ways:
Gut - You just think that they're mafia, without any real reason.
Tone/Motivation/How at ease you are: These are technically distinct items, but they all come more in the player's attitude towards the game than their reads in particular. Mafia are going to be more defensive in general because a mafia dying is a lot worse for the mafia team than a townie dying, and they'll generally be more tense because they are lying to the other players, and they won't be motivated to find the mafia because they already know who they are.
Look at which reads they're pushing: Does it look like they're making reads in order to get a player out of the game/keep another player in the game instead of genuinely trying to find the mafia? In some cases players who don't have consistent reads are mafia. However, if a player strongly pushes a player who is mafia, they're probably town., and conversely if a player avoids making a read on a player who is later revealed to be mafia, that looks somewhat bad.
If a player isn't making reads at all, that's a pretty good sign that they are a mafia, because mafia are aware of who the town and mafia are, and thus aren't able to make reads in the same way as townies. 
Night kill analysis:
This is easier with only one mafia alive, but some people make night kills that other people wouldn't and vice-versa
Meta:
This moreso applies to the experienced players, but if a player is acting differently to how they normally do as town, that generally means that they're likely to be mafia. You don't need to read other games unless you want to, although you can read a previous newbie game for a sense of how things go, no specific reccomendations from me though.

All of the above are really just theoretical, and won't work 100% of the time in practice, but with a combination of various reasons you can become more certain of a player's alignment.

Mechanical information:
If a cop sees that somebody is town/mafia, or a tracker tracks somebody to a sucessful night kill, they are mafia. In general we'll probably talk more about mechanics later, but for now [b]Don't claim your role (for now)[/b]. If you're a power role (i.e. not a vanilla townie), then mafia will kill you. If you're a vanilla townie, then mafia will know that they don't need to kill you if they want to find the power roles.


[b]Ideally, a read should at its core be based on "I think player X is Y alignment because Z", not necessarily in those words, but so long as you convey those three aspects in your post it's a good start. [/b]

Ask me & the other experienced players if you have any questions (even if they're stupid ones) and we'll do our best to answer them.

also take ths [hide=Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)]
Created by EulerNTM
[hide=#]

[b]#-Shot[/b] - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

[b]3P[/b] - [b]Third Party[/b] - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.[/hide]
[hide=A]

[b]AI[/b] - [b]Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

[b]Angleshooting[/b] - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

[b]Anti-spew[/b] - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

[b]AtE[/b] - [b]Appeal to Emotion[/b] - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.[/hide]
[hide=B]

[b]Bandwagon[/b] - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

[b]BM[/b] - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

[b]BP[/b] - [b]Bulletproof[/b] - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

[b]BPV[/b] - [b]Bulletproof Vest[/b] - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

[b]Breadcrumbing[/b] - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

[b]Bussing[/b] - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.[/hide]
[hide=C]
[b]CC[/b] - [b]Counterclaim[/b] - when a player claims the same role as another player.

[b]Cemetery[/b] - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

[b]CFD[/b] - [b]Chinese Fire Drill[/b] - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

[b]Claim Fishing[/b] - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

[b]Crossvoting[/b] - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

[b]Counterwagon[/b] - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.
[/hide]
[hide=D]

[b]D1/D2… etc.[/b] - Day phase

[b]DADV[/b] - [b]Dead Air, Dead Villager[/b] - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

[b]Derpclear[/b] - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

[b]Distancing[/b] - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

[b]DVC[/b] - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.[/hide]
[hide=E]

[b]EOD[/b] - End of Day[/hide]
[hide=F]

[b]F3/F5/F#[/b] - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

[b]Fakeclaiming[/b] - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

[b]Flavor[/b] - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

[b]Fluffposting/Fluffing[/b] - When a player posts nothing of content.

[b]FPS[/b] - [b]Fancy Play Syndrome[/b] - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

[b]Frozen[/b] - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.
[/hide]
[hide=G]

[b]Gambit[/b] - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

[b]Gamethrowing[/b] - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

[b]GOAT[/b] - Greatest of All Time[/hide]
[hide=H]

[b]Hammer[/b] - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

[b]Hard Claim[/b] - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

[b]Hedging[/b] - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

[b]Hydra[/b] - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.[/hide]
[hide=I]

[b]ISO[/b] - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

[b]ITA[/b] - [b]In Thread Attack[/b] - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

[b]ITT[/b] - In The Thread[/hide]
[hide=J]

[b]JOAT[/b] - [b]Jack Of All Trades[/b] - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=K]

[b]KP[/b] - [b]Kill Power[/b] - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.[/hide]
[hide=L]

[b]LHF[/b] - [b]Low Hanging Fruit[/b] - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

[b]Lolcatting[/b] - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

[b]LYLO[/b] - [b]Hang or Lose[/b] - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.[/hide]
[hide=M]

[b]Meta[/b] - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

[b]Modkill[/b] - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

[b]Multi[/b] - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

[b]Multi-ISO[/b] - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

[b]MYLO[/b] - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.[/hide]
[hide=N]

[b]N1/N2… etc.[/b] - Night phase

[b]NAI[/b] - [b]Not Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

[b]NK[/b] - [b]Night Kill[/b] - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

[b]NL[/b] - [b]No Hang[/b] - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.[/hide]
[hide=O]

[b]Openwolving[/b] - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

[b]OMGUS[/b] - [b]Oh My Gosh You Suck[/b] - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.[/hide]
[hide=P]

[b]Peek[/b] - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

[b]Pocketing[/b] - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

[b]Bussing[/b] - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.[/hide]
[hide=C]
[b]CC[/b] - [b]Counterclaim[/b] - when a player claims the same role as another player.

[b]Cemetery[/b] - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

[b]CFD[/b] - [b]Chinese Fire Drill[/b] - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

[b]Claim Fishing[/b] - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

[b]Crossvoting[/b] - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

[b]Counterwagon[/b] - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.
[/hide]
[hide=D]

[b]D1/D2… etc.[/b] - Day phase

[b]DADV[/b] - [b]Dead Air, Dead Villager[/b] - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

[b]Derpclear[/b] - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

[b]Distancing[/b] - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

[b]DVC[/b] - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.[/hide]
[hide=E]

[b]EOD[/b] - End of Day[/hide]
[hide=F]

[b]F3/F5/F#[/b] - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

[b]Fakeclaiming[/b] - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

[b]Flavor[/b] - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

[b]Fluffposting/Fluffing[/b] - When a player posts nothing of content.

[b]FPS[/b] - [b]Fancy Play Syndrome[/b] - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

[b]Frozen[/b] - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.
[/hide]
[hide=G]

[b]Gambit[/b] - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

[b]Gamethrowing[/b] - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

[b]GOAT[/b] - Greatest of All Time[/hide]
[hide=H]

[b]Hammer[/b] - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

[b]Hard Claim[/b] - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

[b]Hedging[/b] - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

[b]Hydra[/b] - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.[/hide]
[hide=I]

[b]ISO[/b] - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

[b]ITA[/b] - [b]In Thread Attack[/b] - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

[b]ITT[/b] - In The Thread[/hide]
[hide=J]

[b]JOAT[/b] - [b]Jack Of All Trades[/b] - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=K]

[b]KP[/b] - [b]Kill Power[/b] - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.[/hide]
[hide=L]

[b]LHF[/b] - [b]Low Hanging Fruit[/b] - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

[b]Lolcatting[/b] - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

[b]LYLO[/b] - [b]Hang or Lose[/b] - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.[/hide]
[hide=M]

[b]Meta[/b] - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

[b]Modkill[/b] - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

[b]Multi[/b] - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

[b]Multi-ISO[/b] - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

[b]MYLO[/b] - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.[/hide]
[hide=N]

[b]N1/N2… etc.[/b] - Night phase

[b]NAI[/b] - [b]Not Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

[b]NK[/b] - [b]Night Kill[/b] - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

[b]NL[/b] - [b]No Hang[/b] - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.[/hide]
[hide=O]

[b]Openwolving[/b] - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

[b]OMGUS[/b] - [b]Oh My Gosh You Suck[/b] - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.[/hide]
[hide=P]

[b]Peek[/b] - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

[b]Pocketing[/b] - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

[b]POE[/b] - [b]Process of Elimination[/b] - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

[b]PR[/b] - [b]Power Role[/b] - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=Q]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=R]

[b]Roleclaim[/b] - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

[b]RVS[/b] - [b]Random Voting Stage[/b] - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

[b]RQS[/b] - [b]Random Questioning Stage[/b] - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

[b]RWSO[/b] - [b]Rand Wolf, Sub Out[/b] - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.[/hide]
[hide=S]

[b]Scum[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players

[b]Scumbaiting[/b] - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

[b]Scumhunting[/b] - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

[b]SOD[/b] - Start of Day

[b]Soft Claim[/b] - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

[b]Spewing[/b] - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).[/hide]
[hide=T]

[b]Thunderdome[/b] - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

[b]Tinfoil[/b] - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

[b]TMI[/b] - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

[b]Tunnel[/b] - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

[b]TWTBAW[/b] - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=U]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=V]

[b]VCA[/b] - [b]Vote Count Analysis[/b] - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

[b]VT[/b] - [b]Vanilla Townie[/b] - A town role with no special abilities.

[b]Vanilla[/b] - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

[b]Villager[/b] - Another word for town-aligned player.[/hide]
[hide=W]

[b]Wagon[/b] - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

[b]WIFOM[/b] - [b]Wine In Front Of Me[/b] - Term originating from [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U_eZmEiyTo0]this scene in The Princess Bride movie[/url]. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

[b]WIL[/b] - [b]Wants It Less[/b] - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

[b]WIM[/b] - [b]Wants It More[/b] - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

[b]WOAT[/b] - Worst of All Time

[b]Wolf[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=X]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Y]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Z]

N/A[/hide]
[/hide]

As for how the game gets started:
Somebody should make RQS, which is a series of around five questions designed to get information from the players, mostly not related to the game (i.e. how to refer to a person), but they also can be game-related. Any of you can do it!

Since the main gameplay loop of mafia is entirely dependent on previous posts, to start somewhere it is common to start RVS, wherein you vote people for random reasons until you eventually get enough things that are actually AI (alignment indicative/readable i.e. something that you can say is towny or wolfy)

That was a bunch of theory all at once but just have fun they don't call it a [i]social[/i] deduction game for nothing or maybe they do?

[b]tl;dr please post & make reads[/b]

please read this before you post (if you see it)
Hi, welcome to the Mafia! forum, my marketing brain is broken so this is all you'll get besides from me today in terms of enthiusasm besides a vague 'mafia is fun' which it is.

You should make sure to read all the rules, and follow them, especially making sure to not edit or delete your posts, or post when it's either night your you are dead.

As for how to play the game:
Mafia is about finding out which players are town, and which ones are mafia. How does this work? Well you look at their posts and you try to see if they're more likely to be a post that town or mafia made. Especially as a newbie it doesn't really matter if you're right so much as that you are making reads. It's really helpful for you to post game-related things, both because it provides interesting ideas into the thread, and it makes yourself more readable, which is something you should want as town. You also want to read all of the posts, which is probably only going to be a few hundred over the course of the game, so that you'll have better reads on each player, and generally understand what is going on.

As for how you tell whether a post is towny/town-indicative or wolfy/scummy/maf-indicative? It's not an exact science, but here are some common ways:
Gut - You just think that they're mafia, without any real reason.
Tone/Motivation/How at ease you are: These are technically distinct items, but they all come more in the player's attitude towards the game than their reads in particular. Mafia are going to be more defensive in general because a mafia dying is a lot worse for the mafia team than a townie dying, and they'll generally be more tense because they are lying to the other players, and they won't be motivated to find the mafia because they already know who they are.
Look at which reads they're pushing: Does it look like they're making reads in order to get a player out of the game/keep another player in the game instead of genuinely trying to find the mafia? In some cases players who don't have consistent reads are mafia. However, if a player strongly pushes a player who is mafia, they're probably town., and conversely if a player avoids making a read on a player who is later revealed to be mafia, that looks somewhat bad.
If a player isn't making reads at all, that's a pretty good sign that they are a mafia, because mafia are aware of who the town and mafia are, and thus aren't able to make reads in the same way as townies.
Night kill analysis:
This is easier with only one mafia alive, but some people make night kills that other people wouldn't and vice-versa
Meta:
This moreso applies to the experienced players, but if a player is acting differently to how they normally do as town, that generally means that they're likely to be mafia. You don't need to read other games unless you want to, although you can read a previous newbie game for a sense of how things go, no specific reccomendations from me though.

All of the above are really just theoretical, and won't work 100% of the time in practice, but with a combination of various reasons you can become more certain of a player's alignment.

Mechanical information:
If a cop sees that somebody is town/mafia, or a tracker tracks somebody to a sucessful night kill, they are mafia. In general we'll probably talk more about mechanics later, but for now Don't claim your role (for now). If you're a power role (i.e. not a vanilla townie), then mafia will kill you. If you're a vanilla townie, then mafia will know that they don't need to kill you if they want to find the power roles.


Ideally, a read should at its core be based on "I think player X is Y alignment because Z", not necessarily in those words, but so long as you convey those three aspects in your post it's a good start.

Ask me & the other experienced players if you have any questions (even if they're stupid ones) and we'll do our best to answer them.

also take ths Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)

You should make sure to read all the rules, and follow them, especially making sure to not edit or delete your posts, or post when it's either night your you are dead.

As for how to play the game:
Mafia is about finding out which players are town, and which ones are mafia. How does this work? Well you look at their posts and you try to see if they're more likely to be a post that town or mafia made. Especially as a newbie it doesn't really matter if you're right so much as that you are making reads. It's really helpful for you to post game-related things, both because it provides interesting ideas into the thread, and it makes yourself more readable, which is something you should want as town. You also want to read all of the posts, which is probably only going to be a few hundred over the course of the game, so that you'll have better reads on each player, and generally understand what is going on.

As for how you tell whether a post is towny/town-indicative or wolfy/scummy/maf-indicative? It's not an exact science, but here are some common ways:
Gut - You just think that they're mafia, without any real reason.
Tone/Motivation/How at ease you are: These are technically distinct items, but they all come more in the player's attitude towards the game than their reads in particular. Mafia are going to be more defensive in general because a mafia dying is a lot worse for the mafia team than a townie dying, and they'll generally be more tense because they are lying to the other players, and they won't be motivated to find the mafia because they already know who they are.
Look at which reads they're pushing: Does it look like they're making reads in order to get a player out of the game/keep another player in the game instead of genuinely trying to find the mafia? In some cases players who don't have consistent reads are mafia. However, if a player strongly pushes a player who is mafia, they're probably town., and conversely if a player avoids making a read on a player who is later revealed to be mafia, that looks somewhat bad.
If a player isn't making reads at all, that's a pretty good sign that they are a mafia, because mafia are aware of who the town and mafia are, and thus aren't able to make reads in the same way as townies.
Night kill analysis:
This is easier with only one mafia alive, but some people make night kills that other people wouldn't and vice-versa
Meta:
This moreso applies to the experienced players, but if a player is acting differently to how they normally do as town, that generally means that they're likely to be mafia. You don't need to read other games unless you want to, although you can read a previous newbie game for a sense of how things go, no specific reccomendations from me though.

All of the above are really just theoretical, and won't work 100% of the time in practice, but with a combination of various reasons you can become more certain of a player's alignment.

Mechanical information:
If a cop sees that somebody is town/mafia, or a tracker tracks somebody to a sucessful night kill, they are mafia. In general we'll probably talk more about mechanics later, but for now Don't claim your role (for now). If you're a power role (i.e. not a vanilla townie), then mafia will kill you. If you're a vanilla townie, then mafia will know that they don't need to kill you if they want to find the power roles.


Ideally, a read should at its core be based on "I think player X is Y alignment because Z", not necessarily in those words, but so long as you convey those three aspects in your post it's a good start.

Ask me & the other experienced players if you have any questions (even if they're stupid ones) and we'll do our best to answer them.

also take ths Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)
Created by EulerNTM
#
#-Shot - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

3P - Third Party - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.

A
AI - Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

Angleshooting - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

Anti-spew - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

AtE - Appeal to Emotion - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.

B
Bandwagon - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

BM - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

BP - Bulletproof - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

BPV - Bulletproof Vest - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

Breadcrumbing - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

Bussing - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.

C
CC - Counterclaim - when a player claims the same role as another player.

Cemetery - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

Claim Fishing - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

Crossvoting - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

Counterwagon - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.

D
D1/D2… etc. - Day phase

DADV - Dead Air, Dead Villager - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

Derpclear - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

Distancing - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

DVC - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.

E
EOD - End of Day

F
F3/F5/F# - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

Fakeclaiming - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

Flavor - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

Fluffposting/Fluffing - When a player posts nothing of content.

FPS - Fancy Play Syndrome - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

Frozen - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.

G
Gambit - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

Gamethrowing - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

GOAT - Greatest of All Time

H
Hammer - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

Hard Claim - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

Hedging - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

Hydra - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.

I
ISO - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

ITA - In Thread Attack - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

ITT - In The Thread

J
JOAT - Jack Of All Trades - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.

K
KP - Kill Power - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.

L
LHF - Low Hanging Fruit - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

Lolcatting - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

LYLO - Hang or Lose - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.

M
Meta - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

Modkill - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

Multi - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

Multi-ISO - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

MYLO - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.

N
N1/N2… etc. - Night phase

NAI - Not Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

NK - Night Kill - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

NL - No Hang - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.

O
Openwolving - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

OMGUS - Oh My Gosh You Suck - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.

P
Peek - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

Pocketing - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

POE - Process of Elimination - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

PR - Power Role - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.

Q
N/A

R
Roleclaim - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

RVS - Random Voting Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

RQS - Random Questioning Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

RWSO - Rand Wolf, Sub Out - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.

S
Scum - Another word for mafia-aligned players

Scumbaiting - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

Scumhunting - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

SOD - Start of Day

Soft Claim - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

Spewing - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).

T
Thunderdome - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

Tinfoil - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

TMI - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

Tunnel - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

TWTBAW - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.

U
N/A

V
VCA - Vote Count Analysis - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

VT - Vanilla Townie - A town role with no special abilities.

Vanilla - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

Villager - Another word for town-aligned player.

W
Wagon - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

WIFOM - Wine In Front Of Me - Term originating from this scene in The Princess Bride movie. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

WIL - Wants It Less - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

WIM - Wants It More - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

WOAT - Worst of All Time

Wolf - Another word for mafia-aligned players.

X
N/A

Y
N/A

Z
N/A


As for how the game gets started:
Somebody should make RQS, which is a series of around five questions designed to get information from the players, mostly not related to the game (i.e. how to refer to a person), but they also can be game-related. Any of you can do it!

Since the main gameplay loop of mafia is entirely dependent on previous posts, to start somewhere it is common to start RVS, wherein you vote people for random reasons until you eventually get enough things that are actually AI (alignment indicative/readable i.e. something that you can say is towny or wolfy)

That was a bunch of theory all at once but just have fun they don't call it a social deduction game for nothing or maybe they do?

tl;dr please post & make reads

[b]RWSO[/b] - [b]Rand Wolf, Sub Out[/b] - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.[/hide]
[hide=S]

[b]Scum[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players

[b]Scumbaiting[/b] - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

[b]Scumhunting[/b] - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

[b]SOD[/b] - Start of Day

[b]Soft Claim[/b] - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

[b]Spewing[/b] - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).[/hide]
[hide=T]

[b]Thunderdome[/b] - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

[b]Tinfoil[/b] - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

[b]TMI[/b] - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

[b]Tunnel[/b] - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

[b]TWTBAW[/b] - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=U]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=V]

[b]VCA[/b] - [b]Vote Count Analysis[/b] - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

[b]VT[/b] - [b]Vanilla Townie[/b] - A town role with no special abilities.

[b]Vanilla[/b] - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

[b]Villager[/b] - Another word for town-aligned player.[/hide]
[hide=W]

[b]Wagon[/b] - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

[b]WIFOM[/b] - [b]Wine In Front Of Me[/b] - Term originating from [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U_eZmEiyTo0]this scene in The Princess Bride movie[/url]. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

[b]WIL[/b] - [b]Wants It Less[/b] - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

[b]WIM[/b] - [b]Wants It More[/b] - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

[b]WOAT[/b] - Worst of All Time

[b]Wolf[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=X]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Y]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Z]

N/A[/hide]
[/hide]

As for how the game gets started:
Somebody should make RQS, which is a series of around five questions designed to get information from the players, mostly not related to the game (i.e. how to refer to a person), but they also can be game-related. Any of you can do it!

Since the main gameplay loop of mafia is entirely dependent on previous posts, to start somewhere it is common to start RVS, wherein you vote people for random reasons until you eventually get enough things that are actually AI (alignment indicative/readable i.e. something that you can say is towny or wolfy)

That was a bunch of theory all at once but just have fun they don't call it a [i]social[/i] deduction game for nothing or maybe they do?

[b]tl;dr please post & make reads[/b][b]please read this before you post (if you see it)[/b]
Hi, welcome to the Mafia! forum, my marketing brain is broken so this is all you'll get besides from me today in terms of enthiusasm besides a vague 'mafia is fun' which it is.

You should make sure to read all the rules, and follow them, especially making sure to not edit or delete your posts, or post when it's either night your you are dead.

As for how to play the game:
Mafia is about finding out which players are town, and which ones are mafia. How does this work? Well you look at their posts and you try to see if they're more likely to be a post that town or mafia made. Especially as a newbie it doesn't really matter if you're right so much as that you are making reads. It's really helpful for you to post game-related things, both because it provides interesting ideas into the thread, and it makes yourself more readable, which is something you should want as town. You also want to read all of the posts, which is probably only going to be a few hundred over the course of the game, so that you'll have better reads on each player, and generally understand what is going on.

As for how you tell whether a post is towny/town-indicative or wolfy/scummy/maf-indicative? It's not an exact science, but here are some common ways:
Gut - You just think that they're mafia, without any real reason.
Tone/Motivation/How at ease you are: These are technically distinct items, but they all come more in the player's attitude towards the game than their reads in particular. Mafia are going to be more defensive in general because a mafia dying is a lot worse for the mafia team than a townie dying, and they'll generally be more tense because they are lying to the other players, and they won't be motivated to find the mafia because they already know who they are.
Look at which reads they're pushing: Does it look like they're making reads in order to get a player out of the game/keep another player in the game instead of genuinely trying to find the mafia? In some cases players who don't have consistent reads are mafia. However, if a player strongly pushes a player who is mafia, they're probably town., and conversely if a player avoids making a read on a player who is later revealed to be mafia, that looks somewhat bad.
If a player isn't making reads at all, that's a pretty good sign that they are a mafia, because mafia are aware of who the town and mafia are, and thus aren't able to make reads in the same way as townies. 
Night kill analysis:
This is easier with only one mafia alive, but some people make night kills that other people wouldn't and vice-versa
Meta:
This moreso applies to the experienced players, but if a player is acting differently to how they normally do as town, that generally means that they're likely to be mafia. You don't need to read other games unless you want to, although you can read a previous newbie game for a sense of how things go, no specific reccomendations from me though.

All of the above are really just theoretical, and won't work 100% of the time in practice, but with a combination of various reasons you can become more certain of a player's alignment.

Mechanical information:
If a cop sees that somebody is town/mafia, or a tracker tracks somebody to a sucessful night kill, they are mafia. In general we'll probably talk more about mechanics later, but for now [b]Don't claim your role (for now)[/b]. If you're a power role (i.e. not a vanilla townie), then mafia will kill you. If you're a vanilla townie, then mafia will know that they don't need to kill you if they want to find the power roles.


[b]Ideally, a read should at its core be based on "I think player X is Y alignment because Z", not necessarily in those words, but so long as you convey those three aspects in your post it's a good start. [/b]

Ask me & the other experienced players if you have any questions (even if they're stupid ones) and we'll do our best to answer them.

also take ths [hide=Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)]
Created by EulerNTM
[hide=#]

[b]#-Shot[/b] - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

[b]3P[/b] - [b]Third Party[/b] - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.[/hide]
[hide=A]

[b]AI[/b] - [b]Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

[b]Angleshooting[/b] - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

[b]Anti-spew[/b] - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

[b]AtE[/b] - [b]Appeal to Emotion[/b] - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.[/hide]
[hide=B]

[b]Bandwagon[/b] - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

[b]BM[/b] - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

[b]BP[/b] - [b]Bulletproof[/b] - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

[b]BPV[/b] - [b]Bulletproof Vest[/b] - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

[b]Breadcrumbing[/b] - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

[b]Bussing[/b] - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.[/hide]
[hide=C]
[b]CC[/b] - [b]Counterclaim[/b] - when a player claims the same role as another player.

[b]Cemetery[/b] - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

[b]CFD[/b] - [b]Chinese Fire Drill[/b] - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

[b]Claim Fishing[/b] - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

[b]Crossvoting[/b] - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

[b]Counterwagon[/b] - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.
[/hide]
[hide=D]

[b]D1/D2… etc.[/b] - Day phase

[b]DADV[/b] - [b]Dead Air, Dead Villager[/b] - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

[b]Derpclear[/b] - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

[b]Distancing[/b] - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

[b]DVC[/b] - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.[/hide]
[hide=E]

[b]EOD[/b] - End of Day[/hide]
[hide=F]

[b]F3/F5/F#[/b] - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

[b]Fakeclaiming[/b] - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

[b]Flavor[/b] - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

[b]Fluffposting/Fluffing[/b] - When a player posts nothing of content.

[b]FPS[/b] - [b]Fancy Play Syndrome[/b] - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

[b]Frozen[/b] - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.
[/hide]
[hide=G]

[b]Gambit[/b] - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

[b]Gamethrowing[/b] - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

[b]GOAT[/b] - Greatest of All Time[/hide]
[hide=H]

[b]Hammer[/b] - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

[b]Hard Claim[/b] - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

[b]Hedging[/b] - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

[b]Hydra[/b] - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.[/hide]
[hide=I]

[b]ISO[/b] - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

[b]ITA[/b] - [b]In Thread Attack[/b] - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

[b]ITT[/b] - In The Thread[/hide]
[hide=J]

[b]JOAT[/b] - [b]Jack Of All Trades[/b] - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=K]

[b]KP[/b] - [b]Kill Power[/b] - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.[/hide]
[hide=L]

[b]LHF[/b] - [b]Low Hanging Fruit[/b] - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

[b]Lolcatting[/b] - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

[b]LYLO[/b] - [b]Hang or Lose[/b] - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.[/hide]
[hide=M]

[b]Meta[/b] - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

[b]Modkill[/b] - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

[b]Multi[/b] - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

[b]Multi-ISO[/b] - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

[b]MYLO[/b] - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.[/hide]
[hide=N]

[b]N1/N2… etc.[/b] - Night phase

[b]NAI[/b] - [b]Not Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

[b]NK[/b] - [b]Night Kill[/b] - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

[b]NL[/b] - [b]No Hang[/b] - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.[/hide]
[hide=O]

[b]Openwolving[/b] - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

[b]OMGUS[/b] - [b]Oh My Gosh You Suck[/b] - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.[/hide]
[hide=P]

[b]Peek[/b] - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

[b]Pocketing[/b] - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

[b]POE[/b] - [b]Process of Elimination[/b] - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

[b]PR[/b] - [b]Power Role[/b] - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=Q]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=R]

[b]Roleclaim[/b] - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

[b]RVS[/b] - [b]Random Voting Stage[/b] - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

[b]RQS[/b] - [b]Random Questioning Stage[/b] - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

[b]Scumhunting[/b] - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.
